message:
refactor(ft-conformance): centralize perf SLO path/schema constants in perf_slo module

The perf SLO binaries each declared their own local `const REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION`,
`DEFAULT_OUTPUT_PATH`, and `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PATH` strings. These were duplicated
verbatim across `bin/run_perf_slos.rs` and referenced by implicit convention
from `bin/check_perf_slos.rs`, creating drift risk: a path change in one binary
would silently break the other (check_perf_slos would be looking at a stale
report path that run_perf_slos no longer writes).

Consolidate all of these into `crates/ft-conformance/src/perf_slo.rs`, which
already owned `REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION`, `DEFAULT_REPORT_PATH`, and the baseline
path constants. Add the missing `DEFAULT_SUMMARY_PATH` and
`DEFAULT_BASELINE_SUMMARY_PATH` next to them so the module is now the single
source of truth for all perf SLO artifact paths and schema versions.

Update both binaries to `use ft_conformance::perf_slo::{...}` instead of
redeclaring locals. Net removal of the duplicated definitions:
- run_perf_slos.rs: drops 3 local consts
- check_perf_slos.rs: drops 1 local const and now resolves `DEFAULT_REPORT_PATH`
  against the same path run_perf_slos writes to

Pure refactor: behavior is unchanged, the resolved strings are identical to
what was in-tree before.
